tools.name: flake8
tools.notes: Configured but NOT used in CI. Config exists but no workflows execute flake8. Running it finds 100+ violations.

linting.notes: No active linting enforcement in CI. The .flake8 config is dead code. Kustomize produces deprecation warnings but these are not treated as errors.

testing.framework: pytest (for Python unit tests) + kustomize build validation + KinD integration tests
testing.mock_patterns: Uses pytest fixtures for HTTP server mocking in pipelines-profile-controller tests
testing.notes: Most tests are integration tests requiring KinD cluster deployment. Only a few unit tests (pipelines-profile-controller sync tests) can run standalone. The main 'test' in CI is 'kustomize build example' which validates manifests build correctly.

gating_checks.name: Component integration tests
gating_checks.command: Various - create KinD cluster, deploy components, validate readiness
gating_checks.notes: 22 separate component test workflows that run when their specific paths change. Each creates a KinD cluster, deploys components, and validates deployment.

advisory_checks.name: Issue Triage
advisory_checks.command: GitHub action for project board management

ci.notes: CI is path-based: workflows only run when specific component paths change. All component tests require full KinD cluster deployment which takes several minutes. No lint checks in CI despite .flake8 config existing.

build.install_command: curl -L https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/releases/download/kustomize%2Fv5.2.1/kustomize_v5.2.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz | tar -xz && chmod +x kustomize && sudo mv kustomize /usr/local/bin/
build.notes: This is a Kubernetes manifests repository. 'Building' means rendering Kustomize manifests to YAML. The main build target is 'example' which includes all components. Individual components can be built with 'kustomize build apps/{component}/upstream/...' or 'kustomize build contrib/{component}/...'

conventions.import_style: Standard Python imports, Kubernetes client library usage
conventions.notes: Manifests use Kustomize conventions: base/ for base configs, overlays/ for variants. Shell scripts in tests/gh-actions/ use kebab-case naming like install_*.sh. YAML manifests follow Kubernetes resource conventions.

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl tar gzip ca-certificates

curl -L https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/releases/download/kustomize%2Fv5.2.1/kustomize_v5.2.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz -o kustomize.tar.gz && tar -xzf kustomize.tar.gz && chmod +x kustomize && mv kustomize /usr/local/bin/

lint_commands.notes: Exits with code 1 - found 100+ violations (mostly E501 line too long). NOT run in CI, dead config.

test_commands.notes: Exit code 0 - SUCCESS. Produces deprecation warnings but builds all manifests successfully. This is the main CI test.
Exit code 0 - SUCCESS. 6 tests passed in 0.28s. These are unit tests that don't require cluster.

container_recipe.notes: Most integration tests require KinD cluster and cannot run in simple container. Only manifest build validation and some Python unit tests work standalone. Full test suite requires: KinD v0.20.0, kubectl, and component deployments (Istio, cert-manager, etc).

results.command: apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl tar gzip ca-certificates
curl + tar to install kustomize v5.2.1

results.output_snippet:
# Warning: 'vars' is deprecated...
# Warning: 'patchesStrategicMerge' is deprecated...
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: auth
results.notes: Build succeeds with deprecation warnings. Warnings are expected and not treated as errors. Produces valid Kubernetes YAML.

results.output_snippet:
./apps/kfp-tekton/.../sync.py:29:80: E501 line too long (103 > 79 characters)
./apps/kfp-tekton/.../sync.py:30:80: E501 line too long (104 > 79 characters)
[100+ more violations]
results.notes: Flake8 found many violations (mostly E501 line too long). However, this is NOT a CI failure - flake8 is not run in any workflow. Config is present but unused.
All 6 unit tests passed. These test the pipelines profile controller sync HTTP endpoint without requiring a cluster.

validation.summary: kustomize build OK (with deprecation warnings), flake8 finds violations but not enforced, pytest unit tests OK (6 passed), integration tests require KinD cluster

gaps: Flake8 configured (.flake8) but never run in CI - dead configuration
No lint enforcement - Python code has 100+ flake8 violations
Most tests are integration tests requiring full KinD cluster deployment
No quick unit test suite that validates logic without infrastructure
Integration tests take 5-15 minutes each due to cluster creation
Kustomize produces deprecation warnings (vars, patchesStrategicMerge, bases) but no plan to fix
Tests cannot run locally without KinD, kubectl, and significant resources (RAM/CPU)
No pre-commit hooks configured despite having test infrastructure
